clean_body: | Peter, Republicans in Washington are once again pushing a budget that would slash Medicaid — and if they succeed, the consequences will be felt right here in California. Medi-Cal, our state’s Medicaid program, helps over 15 million Californians access the care they need. Cuts at the federal level would mean fewer providers, longer wait times, and real harm for working families, seniors, and children. This is personal for me. As a doctor, I’ve treated patients who depend on Medi-Cal — and I know the difference this program makes in people’s lives. That’s why this FEC deadline is so critical. We’re still $4,962 short of our goal, and our numbers will show whether we’re ready to take on this fight and win. 👉 = If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately by pressing a button below. = = = = = We need a House majority that will protect health care — not take it away. I’m doing everything I can to make this a reality, and I’m asking you to stand with me as we close this gap. — Dr. Ami Bera If you’d like to receive less email from our team If you'd like to stop receiving our emails, Paid for by Bera for Congress Bera for Congress PO Box 582496 Elk Grove, CA 95758 United States **

name: Scott Brown
subject: I have something personal to share with you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc
body:
Patriot,

My name is Scott Brown, and I want to take a few minutes to share my story
with you and how the experiences and lessons I’ve learned have led me to make a
big decision this week.
I had a challenging upbringing. My mom was married and divorced four times,
and my dad was married and divorced three times. I was raised by my single mom,
who worked multiple jobs to put food on the table. We didn’t have much, but
with the love and support of my grandparents, we had a fighting chance. We
moved often—I lived in 17 different places before I turned 18.

<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
I know what it’s like to worry about where your next meal is coming from or
whether the bills all get paid.And those early years taught me the value of
grit, of resilience, and hard work.

Nothing in my life has ever been handed to me — I’ve had to fight for every
step forward.

I worked my way through college and law school, taking any odd job and doing
whatever it took to stay afloat.

At 19, I joined the Army National Guard. I served for more than 30 years and
retired as a Colonel. I’ve worn our nation’s uniform with pride and know
firsthand what it means to serve a cause bigger than yourself.

<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
Back in 2010, I took a leap that many thought was crazy. I ran for the U.S.
Senate in Massachusetts as a Republican,driven by a belief that people like you
deserved a real voice in Washington, not just another career politician. I
drove across the state, putting countless miles on my beloved truck, a 2005 GMC
Canyon. With the incredible support of grassroots supporters like you from all
corners of the country,we pulled off a stunning upset and won, shaking the
political establishment to its core.

But that victory was just the start.

I went to Washington as a disruptor, refusing to play the insider games of
career politicians. That made me a target. The national Democrats zeroed in,
recruiting Elizabeth Warren and pouring tens of millions into a campaign to
defeat me. Despite our best efforts, we came up short in that race.

But I wasn’t discouraged. I believed then, and I believe now, that there’s a
bigger plan for all of us.

My wife, Gail, and I became four-time grandparents, and we’ve poured our
hearts into our family and our New Hampshire community. In 2017, when President
Trump asked me to serve as U.S. Ambassador to New Zealand and Samoa,I was
honored to represent our nation, strengthen global partnerships, and protect
American interests abroad.
<https://secure.winred.com/nrsc/doge-hit-ground-running-tc-rs-hf/?recurring=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&address=&zip=&city=&state=&employer_name=&occupation=&amtposition=4>

<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
Today, I’m writing to you with big news: I’m not done fighting. Our country’s
future is at a crossroads, and I’m stepping up again to run for the U.S. Senate
in New Hampshire.I’m doing this for you, for our shared values, and for the
America we believe in. Chip in to Join Scott Brown »
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
You’ve seen what happens when Democrats hold unchecked power...

Runaway inflation...

Chaos at our southern border...

Soft-on-crime policies...

Men in women’s sports and locker rooms...

Foreign policy missteps that weaken our allies and embolden our enemies.

Hardworking Americans like you are being left behind, and we can’t let this
radical agenda take over the Senate in 2026.The stakes are just too high.

Here’s the truth: I can’t do this without you.

The Democrats and their elite allies will flood this race with MILLIONS. It’s
an open seat in a swing state. They don’t want someone like me—someone who
speaks plainly, serves with honor, and refuses to sell out—anywhere near
Washington.

That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, asking you to stand with me from
Day One.

A contribution of $5, $10, or whatever you can give will help us build a
campaign powered by people like you, not political insiders.
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
Every dollar counts, and every voice matters. Chip in to Join Scott Brown »
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>
If you’ve ever felt like your voice doesn’t matter, I’m here to tell you it
does. It matters to me. And it’s the only reason I’m in this fight.

Let’s do this together.

Scott Brown
Candidate for U.S. Senate
New Hampshire Chip in to Join Scott Brown »
<https://secure.winred.com/scott-brown-for-new-hampshire-inc/bio-brown-hf-launch-acs/?utm_medium=pemail&utm_source=rs_aaef_acs&utm_campaign=20250629_bio-launch_sb>

Paid for by Scott Brown for New Hampshire, Inc.

Scott Brown is a retired member of the U.S. Army. Use of his military rank,
job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Army
or the Department of Defense.
You are receiving this email at: %%email%%

PO Box 606
Rye, NH 03870

Privacy Policy <https://scottbrown.com/?page_id=3178> Unsubscribe
<http://links.g4yamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_s=7543cc26c720443082acab6428d487e9&_t=_irSNrnNfC8FZKJVvv3QQczog5cQn3NlaW5Zp25igNGj5R4KH_xtyW6okhgyknMKOvtEHzbJXzLkaquwQ_4EfCpxxgz1IP-OivCYpRj22_pTHaUlVlqSEcarDmOJz0Nt>

body:

_Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110504034/554550213/-1590518309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNGY2NDNjMC0xZDU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kcGstvuVsEYILYa7z0bp8Nf6rhaJJyzxmx2Xg2EnesE=&emci=059859de-8553-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=14f643c0-1d55-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7878003)
Earlier this month, Eric called for Kristi Noem’s resignation — and every single day since, she’s reminded us exactly why she’s unfit to serve. In just the past few weeks, under Noem’s watch as Secretary of Homeland Security, **ICE agents have deported a 4 year-old child battling cancer.** They’ve terrorized our communities, running around like masked bandits and ripping families apart. And they have detained a U.S. Marshal, two members of Congress, and the New York City Comptroller, all for speaking out and defending our right to due process. **This is not law enforcement. I chose option B. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Thank you so much for your help, and enjoy the rest of your weekend. Mohona Chowdhury Campaign Manager Sharice for Congress | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. body:
Peter,

Whether he’s standing up to Donald Trump’s unlawful agenda, holding Purdue Pharma and the Sackler family accountable for fueling the opioid crisis, or championing working families in court, Keith is delivering meaningful wins for the people of Minnesota.

But none of it happens without people like you.

Our end-of-quarter fundraising deadline is less than 48 hours away, and we’re still $15,326 short of our goal. We need an immediate boost to close the gap—but if there’s one thing we know about this grassroots team, it’s that you rise to the challenge when it matters most.

Will you pitch in $10, $25, $50, or whatever works for you to help Keith continue fighting for us?

name: David Hogg
subject: You might hear from me soon
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Nothing beats getting to talk to supporters like you.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250629-1&t=1&akid=2318%2E587540%2EPagzWe)
Hey Peter, it’s David.
I’ve been spending time calling some of our grassroots supporters to say thank you.
**It’s honestly the best part of my week.** I get to connect with the people who are powering this movement. Real people with big hearts who believe in building a better future.
**And I want to make more calls.**
This work isn’t easy. Flipping red seats. Electing generational leaders. Challenging the status quo. Fighting for real change. But when I hear from folks like you, it reminds me we’re not doing this alone.
**We’ve got a major deadline coming up tomorrow and we’re still working to meet our $100,000 goal. So this is the perfect time to give and make an even bigger impact.**
**[If you chip in $5 today, I might give you a call to say thanks. Just a real conversation between two people who care about electing young, progressive leaders.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250629_1_eoq3?refcode=em20250629-2318&amount=5&refcode2=2318_587540_PagzWe&akid=2318%2E587540%2EPagzWe)**

Thank you for your support ahead of this crucial deadline.
Talk soon,
David

You’ve heard the stakes. And now it comes down to this final stretch. The next 24 hours will decide how strong we look heading into July — and how much we can take on as we gear up for the general. subject: She can’t do it alone
body:
| | Peter – I wanted to make sure you saw this email from Marie explaining why tomorrow’s deadline is so important.

Attacks against her have already started rolling in, and this is our first end-of-quarter deadline since our new GOP opponent jumped into the race. All eyes are on Marie.

[The road ahead is long, but I know this grassroots team has what it takes to propel Marie to victory again – but our numbers have to prove it. Rush in $10 or more today to help us meet this crucial goal.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110504408/554551648/947618446?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_30DO_250628_2_11&refcode2=f87b1695-1f55-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODdiMTY5NS0xZjU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=SDxAc1sZNbRD73DwR2H0UpUxo3rEWr7vZ15gjcBQb7A=)

Thanks for standing with Marie.

–Kashia

The road to Election Day won’t be simple. WA-03 has been rated as a “toss-up” race that will decide control of the House next year. The NRCC named me as a top target to defeat, and far-right Super PACs are already running misleading attack ads against me, **including a new $250,000 buy announced just last week.**

[So, we know that all eyes will be on the fundraising totals we report after our next FEC deadline. We need to raise at least another $30,000 before midnight on Monday in order to prove this grassroots team still has what it takes to win again. clean_body: | | Peter – I wanted to make sure you saw this email from Marie explaining why tomorrow’s deadline is so important. Attacks against her have already started rolling in, and this is our first end-of-quarter deadline since our new GOP opponent jumped into the race. All eyes are on Marie. = Thanks for standing with Marie. –Kashia Previous Message from Marie: ICYMI: Our critical Federal Election Commission (FEC) deadline is coming up on June 30th.= = Peter, I firmly believe that we as Americans have more in common than what separates us. That belief is central to my work in Congress: working across the aisle to address the real problems facing families in Southwest Washington – not the manufactured crises and petty political fights that extremist politicians try to distract us with. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = The road to Election Day won’t be simple. WA-03 has been rated as a “toss-up” race that will decide control of the House next year. The NRCC named me as a top target to defeat, and far-right Super PACs are already running misleading attack ads against me, including a new $250,000 buy announced just last week. = | = Thanks so much for pitching in! Marie | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. As the representative for Washington’s third Congressional District, she's working to bring the voices of working people back to DC. Marie works for Washington! = Email is an important way to stay in contact but if you feel like you’re receiving too much, to only receive the most important messages. ** Paid for by MGP Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Marie for Congress, Washington State Democratic Central Committee, and Timber PAC. name: Brent Hennrich
subject: We’re up against Marie’s millions
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITIbIBwsnxP2BavGZnCkEazjG0frzBNzY_suhdPtErBKnGUcJw6UmdMV1bci85I4y67tr-GexkDU_GPFgtq2JBjw8H-3EV4Wlej7RE98XUryQG_CHfsaO0Hh0Gj7ZWYrCoB5FtncnwjA0VBfmRp9VTwihnSjW_B0_gaw5iCFwknJoCB5v81NyjZTV5SvBgtE7H/4hr/BL67mwVLSmqfrRJNh2qJXA/h0/h001.iKZJ52THrnAalR0og6LgN0uNKju5s7H4RPzK1jThpcM)
---
Peter,

My opponent, Marie Gluesenkamp Perez cosplays as a working-class voice in Congress.

But her campaign is bankrolled by hedge fund elites, corporate PACs, and tech bros who spend more on a weekend in D.C. than most families spend on groceries in months.

I'm a working-class dad raising three kids in Southwest Washington. I know what it's like to worry about bills and keeping your kids fed when you've been hit with a layoff. I know what it's like to battle insurance companies just to get basic care.

I'm not running for office to get rich and famous. I'm running because my community asked me to. Because the people making decisions in Congress aren't living the lives they're legislating.
We're up against Marie's millions, and to have a fighting chance, I need a grassroots army at my back.

The end of the quarter fundraising deadline is tomorrow, and we're still a little short of our fundraising goal. This is a big district and we have a lot of ground to cover. The funds we raise in the next few days will determine how far we can go.

[**Pitch in now to help me take on Marie 's donor class and build a people-powered campaign for healthcare, housing, and dignity.**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCvqFPKiRdoyKSGdWznzjmyAHVrExY0Fpodidst0umW2REbPSoF7U4d0PnyAPM5CTdJT_NEHrgXYaSOgZ8ag4qvqdyKZFJaD6d_UyG18optbtrIq1mOzwEr9X1pgQ5-_OMq1PcXChjN36VM4Yfb7NNOlkMI_vgGvGxNUMryVbbx9oFCuHhKbJeN4GSl-rwKpXpSEaGZM4s-8iI7i9VuMf0HsT3kKMGDpuq6c3kkeLngRcejYjpMAkl5fOPvfZL3KnlUFA1qUTqTIRGuXPdr5I0y/4hr/BL67mwVLSmqfrRJNh2qJXA/h1/h001.YYl9njWf8Hc0_Nw8Y44K1Q3lTO6pz4OD7h0QvT0AGv0)
Every dollar helps us get the truth out and challenge the fiction that corporate-funded centrism is the only path forward.

**Let 's Heal Washington!

**Brent

In Congress, I’ve taken on powerful interests to lower housing costs, expand access to affordable health care, and make child care more affordable. I’ll keep up that fight to deliver real relief in the Senate.
We launched this campaign because too many North Carolinians are struggling to afford basic needs while Republicans put billionaires first and leave working families behind. Voters aren’t buying the extremist agenda and deserve a senator who shows up and fights for them every day.

clean_body: Thom Tillis won’t run for reelection in 2026. | = BREAKING NEWS, Peter: = = TRUMP WILL HAND-PICK OUR NEW OPPONENT No matter which MAGA loyalist Trump picks to run in North Carolina, I’m the only Democrat in this race who’s ready to take them on — and win. = | = I’ve done it before. I flipped a district no one thought a Democrat could win, and I did it by building a people-powered movement with people like you. In Congress, I’ve taken on powerful interests to lower housing costs, expand access to affordable health care, and make child care more affordable. I’ll keep up that fight to deliver real relief in the Senate. We launched this campaign because too many North Carolinians are struggling to afford basic needs while Republicans put billionaires first and leave working families behind. Voters aren’t buying the extremist agenda and deserve a senator who shows up and fights for them every day. But this is critical: our campaign is for and by the people — we aren’t funded by special interests or mega corporations. We must win this race and flip the Senate but it depends on grassroots supporters like you stepping up early to show that North Carolinians deserve better and we’re here to fight for it. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue, your donation to Wiley Nickel will process immediately: ** | = = | = = | = = Peter, join us so we can make history again. name: Deb Haaland
subject: They may have the money, but we’ve got the people! ✊🏼
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Every eight years, our governor’s office flips parties. It’s been that way for decades.
Hey there, it's Deb Haaland.
I’ve noticed a trend in New Mexico. Every eight years, our governor’s office flips parties. It’s been that way for decades.
While we’ve made real progress these past eight years, we still have a ways to go. And we know well-funded Republicans — backed by Super PACs and some of the same billionaires who funded Donald Trump — are coming for this seat.
They may have the money, but we’ve got the people! Our strength doesn’t come from a giant war chest; it comes from grassroots power.
That being said, it still takes resources to win statewide. That’s why I’m asking you to make a donation of any amount at the link below to help us reach our $75,000 end-of-quarter goal:
[**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250629_2_eoq**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250629_2_eoq?refcode=em20250629-2921&refcode2=2921_400544_sx9WY0&akid=2921%2E400544%2Esx9WY0)
With an outgoing Democratic governor, Republicans have the historical trends on their side this year. It won’t be easy, but I believe we can buck that trend by building a movement grounded in shared values that transcends party lines.
Every dollar you give will help us keep the governor’s office blue and build a future where every New Mexico family can thrive.
Thank you, as always, for your support.
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250629-2&t=1&refcode2=2921_400544_sx9WY0&akid=2921%2E400544%2Esx9WY0)**

And our biggest Tuesday of protests yet! | | | | | | | | | | | | Our movement is powered by hope and joy and you're invited to share both, along with some tasty food, at our Resistance Beach Party on July 6th! Sign up now and we’ll save you a spot! | | And this Tuesday will be our biggest Tesla Takedown Tuesday yet with NINE events beginning at 8 AM at two overpasses and continuing throughout the day. With MAGA about to pass their disastrous Big Ugly Bill, feel free to bring signs in support of Medicaid and other key programs. We should be funding healthcare, not billionaires! See you out there! — Devin | | | | | | | | | | | | | www.devinhermanson.com Paid for by Devin for Progress |

Peter, Republicans think we’re going to tune out and “get over” millions of people losing health care. **But we know better,_and we're not backing down. _** Right now, they’re feeling the pressure. Donald Trump is losing court case after court case -– proving his actions are illegal and unconstitutional. Americans across the country are protesting in their communities and at town halls. And already in 2025, Democrats are breaking voter turnout records and winning special elections in deep red seats. We have to keep this momentum going and flip the House next year, and we need **your** help. With our end-of-quarter deadline tomorrow, your support is more critical than ever. Every contribution sends a message that we're not going to sit idly by while Republicans threaten our health care or rip away food assistance from families. **We're taking action, and together, we can elect Democrats committed to putting people over politics.** **[Can you chip in today to ensure we have the resources to keep up the fight? clean_body: | = Peter, Republicans think we’re going to tune out and “get over” millions of people losing health care. But we know better,and we're not backing down. Right now, they’re feeling the pressure. Donald Trump is losing court case after court case -– proving his actions are illegal and unconstitutional. Americans across the country are protesting in their communities and at town halls. And already in 2025, Democrats are breaking voter turnout records and winning special elections in deep red seats. We have to keep this momentum going and flip the House next year, and we need your help. With our end-of-quarter deadline tomorrow, your support is more critical than ever. Every contribution sends a message that we're not going to sit idly by while Republicans threaten our health care or rip away food assistance from families. We're taking action, and together, we can elect Democrats committed to putting people over politics. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you for standing with us. Team,
With the primary election behind us, I'm officially up against a MAGA-endorsed extremist. Before I talk about how terrible Jason Miyares is for Virginia, can I ask you to chip in $10 towards our first end-of-quarter deadline of the general election? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-launch_em_q22025?amount=10]
CHIP IN $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-launch_em_q22025?amount=10]
Trump’s legal violations are egregious. He’s spent months attacking the foundations of our democracy – from giving DOGE access to our private data to disregarding the Constitution.
And Virginia’s AG Jason Miyares has failed to stand up to Trump. He’s spent his time criticizing DEI programs while ignoring how Trump’s policies have actually hurt the people of the Commonwealth.
Miyares would rather attack Virginia’s most vulnerable communities than stand up for the rule of law. But as Attorney General, I will stand up to Trump and his allies and defend our laws and Constitution.
Our first end-of-quarter deadline of the general election is TOMORROW NIGHT. Your support has never been as critical as it is right now. Chip in to my campaign today so we can start the final phase of this campaign on the right foot. >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-launch_em_q22025]

| | I have an important update for you, Fellow American. Poll after poll shows that my race for Governor is a toss-up!

Fellow American,

For months, Texas lawmakers were under a fundraising freeze while the legislative session was underway. That means no campaign dollars raised. Not a single cent.

We were fighting to deliver the largest school choice program in the nation, protect our elections, secure property tax relief, ban foreign adversaries from buying Texas land, keep criminals behind bars, and fund border security.

**But while we were working to make Texas safer and stronger,_the Democrat Machine was busy plotting._**

They didn't pause for a second. They spent that time organizing, mobilizing out-of-state liberals, and laying the groundwork to flip Texas blue.

**[🚨Now, the freeze has lifted and we have the shortest window in modern history to catch up and show the strength of our movement before the End-of-Quarter fundraising deadline.🚨](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=872a385f0f344fa79b0eca8f8199ac83&_e=bWO1OrVr_bbjNlPgIHzRmbuJsjlkzVP3roHbH3mVNffmUtsSMK_Kkyy9_NCLlsFKj4TFoCsAmhhUfnDqgkHrdW3q10sjwLumehpfutZ5dxkqXDRWAkMckYG9PZ13HbTPwPGu3GmHw3hEwucYXcWSfAOX_A_GGS0aSI8xqBzvK9uUPbg0ggtRV1pFVNHe2qj32bK3ugay3iw1yVwUubiCz1wQR060FpMNeC1aD0GnH7_KoaOz8KEd9rF1xZeJrMbiL5KsWYFTL3exKX7MWhDt4OYTA3EWvMF8PmL16Xu9auDQtIRXjQZ7gYYhs7F7oT3tgsz6N5Asj4CGfeEnPMIMpS9Q9022PHkdqJ2uZstVX8N90P1PWkwayyD0CuqxsX5OAnCaM5rtziyv_E9oaEBb1_L6g4Tnsd4Ae8oGMs1U4V7T0A4BpSGkR15s3PercwOWgSawC3MWhbljBg6cgG3pKpVHHJdAaz8gfEP7Hvczk2lyeI5V_iU_17HFvnYnCDcQV2u95WN5XYzOvbSAoK6AsMoGjixByMK0Ox8VMZUXarKuN879oB9WbMUSj11s6pn8Kj_2_IZDuowd-iYcDC1GS-S2viPpJZN26-rwU9xV6G4_RV_QwIq5YgTKaGUB7N8pFHjErlqHTetcy11_7oZPpFycIi-jcUl8TKT4oxBvbIL01EPPue1uFUaMv3uvK9eBm8OPp8RntB-xU6GXmP6ukg%3D%3D)**

**[Will you rush in a donation today to help us surge past our goal before June ends? Your donation of any amount will make a BIG impact with so little time. >>](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=872a385f0f344fa79b0eca8f8199ac83&_e=bWO1OrVr_bbjNlPgIHzRmbuJsjlkzVP3roHbH3mVNffmUtsSMK_Kkyy9_NCLlsFKj4TFoCsAmhhUfnDqgkHrdW3q10sjwLumehpfutZ5dxkqXDRWAkMckYG9PZ13HbTPwPGu3GmHw3hEwucYXcWSfAOX_A_GGS0aSI8xqBzvK9uUPbg0ggtRV1pFVNHe2qj32bK3ugay3iw1yVwUubiCz1wQR060FpMNeC1aD0GnH7_KoaOz8KEd9rF1xZeJrMbiASQdjSUG7z2W1fV18nR3QdDJSymBKRYdRodkitsrcMELkC8TRQ-J_ixL3MrprgBc79B_EeMRKsEuApfq7Hm-E5qwCSZJ3xIQNTESohF0d21464aXylikIn8Tbw3Z0o-1DLlIrOzJxjjgUnA5Tq24yadGzXEcIc7iCMyo1ijGcxX0vKGHUYSiZFBjsjU50qwFXMPWx3rclOAH4e2I72GeoRVAzb7dk2sJ-39q5UoVymUyt0vNuTjtXskIujyBTG745oDZwd1AQa7vSZyfXsWlYhhL3Jql7duBlKCqF7xGsSWzgGIfPksZUWThsYl1fU97d-6fDGnfvD2bG4fnHQ09iv0Ye9RyHSBRcDOItROfIy70EYxsK3pc0r6rIlcC0amMbvcF_fqV33zMCr5DtGmso7qQvVxYZavNGGA5Z8jnNEIgIFg3wO1CxBkCpV4BLZOt140TpA71T2zrO0Zq75jm7Q%3D%3D)**

The Radical Left refuses to give up on Texas because they know we're the front line in the battle for conservative values.

We cannot let them out-organize or out-work us. Not here. Not now.

Texas has always stood strong. But this moment requires everyone in the fight. Every dollar matters. Every voice counts. **[Can I count on your support today?](http://links.conservativesfordjt.com/u/click?_t=05b03ef2209247bcbdad7fd5099cd670&_m=872a385f0f344fa79b0eca8f8199ac83&_e=bWO1OrVr_bbjNlPgIHzRmbuJsjlkzVP3roHbH3mVNffmUtsSMK_Kkyy9_NCLlsFKj4TFoCsAmhhUfnDqgkHrdW3q10sjwLumehpfutZ5dxkqXDRWAkMckYG9PZ13HbTPwPGu3GmHw3hEwucYXcWSfAOX_A_GGS0aSI8xqBzvK9uUPbg0ggtRV1pFVNHe2qj32bK3ugay3iw1yVwUubiCz1wQR060FpMNeC1aD0GnH7_KoaOz8KEd9rF1xZeJrMbi_SNmXfztHD-AkpoSU6kZcd5YGNFeyK-pjl1HgzTpRsyFoabymwJbPeEN88ueyj9s_oDLiTReSi1phwnty2xEhSahnXsIrAKypctnNPj3EQ4P1B4Kmjft8B_itx56wTkwjLlXLBe9vF7mfXCIiVdURh_r6SkKJsanJtLbsC2cn28l8qPhQq3QoUfTOuXbQAYTwbk2PbsUB5OUzJSmCbcMCq5mZX4hVTxT8-VjQXsvXFNp-XL0ArAF3Rw1QBOivkTC94-asfgfFoJjClxzQ7620npOlSwbN0HblGi5iBaJgMsM_vn5nlA3EFWAwsk2O3myH3BqqXGcax7ANFsVZUBHVZoja0mUy0a7Z60cXcdGi8uumkIoVibcOR4fnOj0UQhSrBbJe49f_h9Hq73nBxarVj23mJbgkQ0UMjajSVN3m141m0aYevTzp9o27RI7w1N2s-_QnO8rVx27ZJtHjLi3_A%3D%3D)**

They spent that time organizing, mobilizing out-of-state liberals, and laying the groundwork to flip Texas blue. ##### _**You have to wonder what the men behind him were thinking then ...and now**_

**BY BRIAN KAREM (**_**whose**_****_**podcast and Substack, "[Just Ask The Question,](https://substack.com/redirect/f6986c56-ea7f-498c-9c5c-3db9b62f25ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" and related Substack "[Reporter's Notebook](https://substack.com/redirect/bcd517b6-dbdb-4a21-a0f6-2cc2be2dd549?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" are both Must Subscribes!)**_
So we've now had a week to look at Donald Trump's big wartime President moment. It feels like just the other day Trump was begging for a Nobel Peace Prize. oh, that's right, because it was.
That's not an exaggeration. One of Trump's greatest bragging lines has been that he's anti-war. So, after he attacked Iran, he tried to frame it as a move for "peace," and then he posted a lengthy diatribe on Truth Social making several claims - mostly false - about why he deserves a Nobel Peace Prize:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5ff07dc9-8b53-4508-8dbe-3907de535b85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But here's the inconvenient truth Trump likes to forget (like most truths); Trump walked away from a negotiated deal in 2015 signed off on by five permanent members of the U.N. Security council and Germany, designed to prevent this very scenario from occurring.
So, Trump has yet again proven he's an arsonist who wants to take credit for being a fireman. He's still the same grifter as he's been every second of every day of his life, and everything that occurred in Iran came as a direct result of Trump scuttling that Iran deal President Barack Obama helped to negotiate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/542119ac-6bfa-4559-8f20-abcf850639da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Trump went about destroying everything that Obama did because he didn't like Obama. He didn't care about the consequences and has tried, since coming back in office, to re-negotiate the very deal that was signed in 2015. But Iran didn't trust Trump, so there was no deal to be had by the so-called dealmaker.
Trump is responsible for all of it - but that's not how he's selling it, of course. Here is some of what Trump had to say:
* * *
_**"Iran, the bully of the Middle East, must now make peace. If they do not, future attacks will be far greater and a lot easier.**_
_**"For years, Iran has been saying death to America, death to Israel. They have been killing our people, blowing off their arms, blowing off their legs with roadside bombs. That was their specialty. We lost over a thousand people and hundreds of thousands throughout the Middle East and around the world have died as a direct result of their hate.**_
_**"In particular, so many were killed by their general, Qasem Soleimani. I decided a long time ago that I would not let this happen. It will not continue. I want to thank and congratulate Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu. We worked as a team. Like perhaps no team has ever worked before. And we've gone a long way to erasing this horrible threat to Israel. "**_
* * *
Trump's speech was riddled with his usual hyperbole, but by saying that he decided long ago "I would not let this happen," the question must be asked: _Mr. President were you lying when you called yourself an anti-war president_ , _or are you lying now?_
With Trump it's probably both.
Trump's threats and self aggrandizement were also on full display. It's obvious he was hoping to make a splash that would rival the speech President Obama made when he announced the U.S. had killed Osama Bin Laden.
But he forgot he's Donald Trump. So his bluster got in the way again.
* * *
_**"With all of that being said, this cannot continue. There will be either peace or there will be tragedy for Iran, far greater than we have witnessed over the last eight days. Remember, there are many targets left. Tonight's was the most difficult of them all by far, and perhaps the most lethal. But if peace does not come quickly, we will go after those other targets with precision, speed and skill. Most of them can be taken out in a matter of minutes.**_
_**"There is no military in the world that could have done what we did tonight. Not even close. There has never been a military that could do what took place just a little while ago.**_
* * *
This came from a man who used "bone spurs" to avoid military service during the Vietnam war. It is harder to take Trump praising the military seriously now, considering how he has treated the military and veterans from cutting their benefits to insulting their memories.
When Trump canceled a visit to the Aisne-Marne American Cemetery, near Paris, in 2018, he blamed rain for the last-minute decision, saying that "the helicopter couldn't fly" and that the Secret Service wouldn't drive him there. Neither claim was true.
"Why should I go to that cemetery? It's filled with losers." In a separate conversation on the same trip, Trump referred to the more than 1,800 marines who lost their lives at Belleau Wood as "suckers," for getting killed.

Among his other notable gems:
#### **"I was never a big fan of John McCain, disagreed with him on many things including ridiculous endless wars and the lack of success he had in dealing with the VA**
#### **"He's a war hero because he was captured. I like people that weren't captured, okay? I hate to tell you."[July 18, 2015](https://substack.com/redirect/2ff55dd2-6d87-4f41-adaf-ed96c04348a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
#### **"Look, I don't want any wounded guys in the parade. This doesn't look good for me."[Summer 2017](https://substack.com/redirect/42ca47c3-7394-4418-80a9-8f48181886a9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
* * *
Trump has denied having made even more disparaging comments about the military and is now trying to rebrand himself as a warrior and patriot. And now we're at war. Not a declared war.
Like every other President since World War II, he just did went ahead and did it and Congress, which has exercised none of its Constitutional Authority--Mike Johnson is literally asleep at the switch--is complicit in letting him get away with it. This occasion is not unique in that regard.
Apparently at least one veterans group was unhappy with Trump's unilateral decision to bomb a country with which we weren't at war:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b7aad4f8-2886-4443-a8fb-a7d702c5d9f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But Senator Lindsey Graham and other Trump allies were quick to circle the wagons around the President, right after the bombing and even more so in the week since, as he's taken criticism from the Left and the Right
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/13d918c1-45f3-4d23-ad85-a12b39168466?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Iran, of course, said the strikes had no effect and warnings were extended to American servicemen on bases overseas to be prepared for a counter attack. Drones and missiles are to be expected from one the largest sponsors of terrorism in the world - and the threat of terrorism extends to U.S. territory.
Trump may have said the strikes were an overwhelming success, but as often as Trump has lied about anything and everything, you cannot take him at face value. Nor can you take the Iranian government at face value. There is a history there that precludes trust on both sides.
But as last week wore on it became clear, via objective observers, that little was accomplished. Which begs the question, how will Trump follow up? And beyond the expected condemnations and lickspittle Lindsey Grahams, what will other domestic interests do?
For example, many Arab-American groups in the United States helped put Donald Trump in the White House. They voted for him as protesters chanted "Genocide Joe has to go" at various events across the country, in reference to former President Joe Biden who they claimed was "too close" to Israel and Benjamin Netanyahu.
Trump's statement pledging how close a "team" he and Netanyahu are will not easily be forgotten by the Arab-American community. But Trump either didn't figure that into the calculus of today's events or didn't care to. He already got what he wanted from them.
The only thing for certain is that the world just got a lot less safe in the short run. No one knows what it will bring long term and Trump's demands for immediate peace - to the Middle Eastern mind is a staggering bit of grandiose drama.
"To them it will be like a bully smacking you to the ground and saying 'Give up!' That's not how it works," a member of the Department of Defense told me on background.
> The question isn't 'if' Iran will strike, but how and when and what Trump will do afterward. This could escalate and get out of control very quickly, or it could end just as quickly - but I would not bet on that
All we can do is hold our breath and wait. So much for the anti-war president.
_Brian Karem is an award-winning reporter, columnist, and author of "Free the Press," and seven others books. He has a popular podcast and Substack, "[Just Ask The Question,](https://substack.com/redirect/f6986c56-ea7f-498c-9c5c-3db9b62f25ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" as well as a related Substack "[Reporter's Notebook](https://substack.com/redirect/bcd517b6-dbdb-4a21-a0f6-2cc2be2dd549?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." **You SHOULD subscribe to BOTH**_

He claims victory, demands peace, dares Iran, curses out/threatens Israel and Iran, all in one week--after he walked away from a deal that would've prevented all of this | | | Jun 29 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- You have to wonder what the men behind him were thinking then ...and now `Full Blue Amp Subscription For Just $1.32/Week!` We made the decision to give all who want it full access to Blue Amp for only $1.32 per week! Full access to Blue Amp for only $1.32 per week is part of our plan to build out a Creators ' Fund, to give grants to smaller creators to build an indy-media ecosystem Amplify Good. Support Indy Media BY BRIAN KAREM (whose**podcast and Substack, "" and related Substack "" are both Must Subscribes!) So we've now had a week to look at Donald Trump's big wartime President moment. It feels like just the other day Trump was begging for a Nobel Peace Prize. oh, that's right, because it was. That's not an exaggeration. One of Trump's greatest bragging lines has been that he's anti-war. So, after he attacked Iran, he tried to frame it as a move for "peace," and then he posted a lengthy diatribe on Truth Social making several claims - mostly false - about why he deserves a Nobel Peace Prize: | | ---|---|--- But here's the inconvenient truth Trump likes to forget (like most truths); Trump walked away from a negotiated deal in 2015 signed off on by five permanent members of the U.N. Security council and Germany, designed to prevent this very scenario from occurring. So, Trump has yet again proven he's an arsonist who wants to take credit for being a fireman. He's still the same grifter as he's been every second of every day of his life, and everything that occurred in Iran came as a direct result of Trump scuttling that Iran deal President Barack Obama helped to negotiate. | | ---|---|--- Trump went about destroying everything that Obama did because he didn't like Obama. He didn't care about the consequences and has tried, since coming back in office, to re-negotiate the very deal that was signed in 2015. But Iran didn't trust Trump, so there was no deal to be had by the so-called dealmaker. Trump is responsible for all of it - but that's not how he's selling it, of course. Here is some of what Trump had to say: "Iran, the bully of the Middle East, must now make peace. If they do not, future attacks will be far greater and a lot easier. "For years, Iran has been saying death to America, death to Israel. They have been killing our people, blowing off their arms, blowing off their legs with roadside bombs. That was their specialty. We lost over a thousand people and hundreds of thousands throughout the Middle East and around the world have died as a direct result of their hate. "In particular, so many were killed by their general, Qasem Soleimani. I decided a long time ago that I would not let this happen. It will not continue. I want to thank and congratulate Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu. We worked as a team. Like perhaps no team has ever worked before. And we've gone a long way to erasing this horrible threat to Israel. " Trump's speech was riddled with his usual hyperbole, but by saying that he decided long ago "I would not let this happen," the question must be asked: Mr. President were you lying when you called yourself an anti-war president , or are you lying now? With Trump it's probably both. Trump's threats and self aggrandizement were also on full display. It's obvious he was hoping to make a splash that would rival the speech President Obama made when he announced the U.S. had killed Osama Bin Laden. But he forgot he's Donald Trump. So his bluster got in the way again. "With all of that being said, this cannot continue. There will be either peace or there will be tragedy for Iran, far greater than we have witnessed over the last eight days. Remember, there are many targets left. Tonight's was the most difficult of them all by far, and perhaps the most lethal. But if peace does not come quickly, we will go after those other targets with precision, speed and skill. Most of them can be taken out in a matter of minutes. "There is no military in the world that could have done what we did tonight. Not even close. There has never been a military that could do what took place just a little while ago. This came from a man who used "bone spurs" to avoid military service during the Vietnam war. It is harder to take Trump praising the military seriously now, considering how he has treated the military and veterans from cutting their benefits to insulting their memories. When Trump canceled a visit to the Aisne-Marne American Cemetery, near Paris, in 2018, he blamed rain for the last-minute decision, saying that "the helicopter couldn't fly" and that the Secret Service wouldn't drive him there. Neither claim was true. "Why should I go to that cemetery? It's filled with losers." In a separate conversation on the same trip, Trump referred to the more than 1,800 marines who lost their lives at Belleau Wood as "suckers," for getting killed. `Full Blue Amp Subscription For Just $1.32/Week!` Full access to Blue Amp for only $1.32 per week is part of our plan to build out a Creators ' Fund, to give grants to smaller creators to build an indy-media ecosystem Get the truth corporate media ignores. From a former Biden ad man & former Emmy-winning MSNBC anchor who know how DC works & share what's behind the curtain Amplify Good. Support Indy Media Among his other notable gems: "I was never a big fan of John McCain, disagreed with him on many things including ridiculous endless wars and the lack of success he had in dealing with the VA "He's a war hero because he was captured. I like people that weren't captured, okay? I hate to tell you.". "Look, I don't want any wounded guys in the parade. This doesn't look good for me.". Trump has denied having made even more disparaging comments about the military and is now trying to rebrand himself as a warrior and patriot. And now we're at war. Not a declared war. Like every other President since World War II, he just did went ahead and did it and Congress, which has exercised none of its Constitutional Authority--Mike Johnson is literally asleep at the switch--is complicit in letting him get away with it. This occasion is not unique in that regard. Apparently at least one veterans group was unhappy with Trump's unilateral decision to bomb a country with which we weren't at war: | | ---|---|--- But Senator Lindsey Graham and other Trump allies were quick to circle the wagons around the President, right after the bombing and even more so in the week since, as he's taken criticism from the Left and the Right | | ---|---|--- Iran, of course, said the strikes had no effect and warnings were extended to American servicemen on bases overseas to be prepared for a counter attack. Drones and missiles are to be expected from one the largest sponsors of terrorism in the world - and the threat of terrorism extends to U.S. territory. Trump may have said the strikes were an overwhelming success, but as often as Trump has lied about anything and everything, you cannot take him at face value. Nor can you take the Iranian government at face value. There is a history there that precludes trust on both sides. But as last week wore on it became clear, via objective observers, that little was accomplished. Which begs the question, how will Trump follow up? And beyond the expected condemnations and lickspittle Lindsey Grahams, what will other domestic interests do? For example, many Arab-American groups in the United States helped put Donald Trump in the White House. They voted for him as protesters chanted "Genocide Joe has to go" at various events across the country, in reference to former President Joe Biden who they claimed was "too close" to Israel and Benjamin Netanyahu. Trump's statement pledging how close a "team" he and Netanyahu are will not easily be forgotten by the Arab-American community. But Trump either didn't figure that into the calculus of today's events or didn't care to. He already got what he wanted from them. The only thing for certain is that the world just got a lot less safe in the short run. No one knows what it will bring long term and Trump's demands for immediate peace - to the Middle Eastern mind is a staggering bit of grandiose drama. "To them it will be like a bully smacking you to the ground and saying 'Give up!' That's not how it works," a member of the Department of Defense told me on background. The question isn't 'if' Iran will strike, but how and when and what Trump will do afterward. This could escalate and get out of control very quickly, or it could end just as quickly - but I would not bet on that All we can do is hold our breath and wait. So much for the anti-war president. name: Tobias Read
subject: trust in our democratic institutions is under strain
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em]
Hi, folks. It’s Oregon Secretary of State, Tobias Read.
At a moment when trust in our democratic institutions is under strain, it’s critical that our elected officials lead with integrity, transparency, and courage.
That’s what I’ve tried to do each and every day here in Oregon since I became Secretary of State.
But our democracy is facing relentless and well-funded attacks and misinformation each and every day. Worst of all, these bad actors are counting on us to give up the fight and give in to their attacks.
We can’t let that happen. That’s why I’m reaching out to you personally.
My team is just 24 hours away from our critical end-of-quarter fundraising deadline, and we need your help to raise the funds required to continue fighting for Oregon’s elections. Can I count on you to join the fight and contribute to the cause with a grassroots contribution of any amount right away? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em]
Our democracy works best when everyone has a voice — and when elections are secure, transparent, and accessible.
As Secretary of State, I’m fighting to put people before politics, ensure every vote counts, and protect every voter’s voice in this democracy.
But here's the truth: Helping elect pro- democracy candidates takes resources, and with our end-of-quarter fundraising deadline just hours away, I need your help and support.
So please, can you chip in before midnight to help me stand strong for democracy, voting rights, and transparency? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=250629em]
Thank you,
Tobias Read
Oregon Secretary of State
P.S. Don’t forget! The Oregon Political Tax Credit allows you to donate $50 (if you file single) or $100 (if you file joint) to a candidate, PAC, or party, which will be fully credited back as a rebate — so it’s free to donate! For more information, click here. [https://www.oregon.gov/omd/ONG/Documents/SMFS/Personal%20Financial%20Counselor/Political-Tax-Credit.pdf?]

| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO) **Listen up:** | **WE TOLD YOU:** Pete Buttigieg, Jamie Raskin, Jasmine Crockett, Joe Biden, and Elizabeth Warren all want Washington, DC to become the 51st STATE!

**WE TOLD YOU:** If DC became the 51st state, 700,000 Americans would get representation in Congress AND we’d gain 2 more Democratic Senators!

**WE TOLD YOU:** Donald Trump and the GOP HATE the idea of DC becoming a state because it would **WRECK their far-right agenda.** **So, we’re rushing to gather 20,000 signatures before 11:59 PM so that we can reach our goal and PRESSURE CONGRESS TO MAKE DC THE 51ST STATE!**
---

[**we’ve only got 8 hours to reach our midnight goal. Can you sign on now to stand with Top Democrats??**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/63325?t=3&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO) []()
[ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/63325?t=4&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO)
---
Listen – We’re counting on Top Democrats like you to step up and help support our campaign for DC Statehood.

DC has a larger population than Vermont and Wyoming but they have absolutely 0 voting representation in Congress... It's disgusting!

**Trump and his GOP are opposed to DC Statehood because they’re afraid of Democracy and giving power back to the people!**

But, we can’t stand by and idly watch for another second, that’s why we’re penning a massive petition.
[**20,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED BY MIDNIGHT: Add your name to our MASSIVE petition to demand Congress make DC the 51st state! >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/63325?t=5&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO) [ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/63325?t=6&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO)
---
Let’s get this done,

_Fight for Reform _ | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com
[ _Click here to get fewer emails._](https://act.fightforreform.org/signup/Less-Emails?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=7&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO) | [_Click here to unsubscribe._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-2022?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=8&akid=23176%2E4449941%2E24H-nO)

With your support we can harness the power of our grassroots supporters to influence decision makers and put control back in the hands of the people.

name: Rebecca Cooke
subject: Who do you want fighting for you, Peter?
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506169/554557686/1706257898?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWZmOTk4NC0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q8oOBdfcOLV7thviPINSldkVepSazS-TSvibkn1yILg=&emci=66ff9984-2755-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=6eff9984-2755-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=5944223)
Peter, [**I wanted to share more with you about my background in WI-03 and why I’m running for Congress. I hope you’ll read the whole message, and then consider making a $3 donation ahead of our critical June FEC deadline to help us improve the lives of working families here in Wisconsin.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506170/554557687/-2087246159?refcode=em-6.29.25-all-2-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWZmOTk4NC0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q8oOBdfcOLV7thviPINSldkVepSazS-TSvibkn1yILg=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506171/554557688/-2087246159?refcode=em-6.29.25-all-2-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWZmOTk4NC0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q8oOBdfcOLV7thviPINSldkVepSazS-TSvibkn1yILg=) I grew up on a farm in Chippewa county that raised registered dairy cattle – Prairie Belle Holsteins. I practically lived at the fair each summer showing cows in 4-H throughout the region. My family has had a farm in this district for 150 years – six generations. Growing up in Western Wisconsin, you learn quickly about the importance of hard work, personal responsibility, honesty and loyalty. Even when times were tough, we were brought up to give the shirts off of our backs to help neighbors in need. That sense of community is where Derrick Van Orden and I aremost different: **While I’m a 6th generation Western Wisconsinite and come from a long line of dairy farmers** , Derrick Van Orden went Hollywood before settling down here. **While I’m building a broad and diverse coalition of grassroots supporters** , Derrick Van Orden relies on corporate PAC support and dark money to keep this seat. **And while I’m using my lived experience to advocate for the needs of working families and rural Wisconsin** , Derrick Van Orden is cutting our lifelines to appease Elon Musk and the billionaires trying to hijack our country. I'm not just running against Derrick Van Orden — I’m running for Congress to fight for Wisconsin’s communities, put people first, and protect our way of life. This is about service, decency, and getting things done. [**The residents of Wisconsin’s 3rd District need real, hardworking, local leadership to fight for them. That’s why I’m hoping you’ll contribute $3 before our end-of-quarter deadline. Can I count on you to stand with us in the fight?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506172/554557689/-2087246159?refcode=em-6.29.25-all-2-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWZmOTk4NC0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q8oOBdfcOLV7thviPINSldkVepSazS-TSvibkn1yILg=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | ****[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506173/554557690/-2087246159?refcode=em-6.29.25-all-2-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZWZmOTk4NC0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=q8oOBdfcOLV7thviPINSldkVepSazS-TSvibkn1yILg=)****

name: Tina Kotek
subject: Anything?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Hey there, Peter — [_**Can I convince you to pitch in $26.73 before our end-of-quarter deadline? Here’s why your support is so important:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110506317/554558288/-1018398773?amount=26.73&refcode=AU_TK_FR_GEN_EOQ_AC_20250629_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMGNkOGMzOS0yODU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9wNBW7NBsTA-BjI_XjVVgiJ4aKS5otK9HgNUtnqbVtc=) Donald Trump is on a mission to gut the services that everyday people across Oregon rely on so the billionaires who backed his campaign can buy _another_ yacht. The vast majority of Oregonians are _extremely_ opposed to the cuts in Trump’s budget — his budget hurts rural communities, marginalized communities, and people who are struggling to get by. I’m using every tool in my toolbox to advocate for Oregonians and fight for a better future,_so the last thing on my mind is our campaign’s fundraising._ [_**But truthfully, Peter, our campaign is only able to reach Oregonians thanks to grassroots contributions from folks just like yourself. name: Ellen R. Malcolm, EMILYs List
subject: My reasons for hope:
body:
|
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/110506299/554558079/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE667_20250629_ERMRS_ALL&sourceid=1056479&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYTYxNTlkMi0yNzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jaISHWHnpsS6iHok9LN_hUxDUFgFlXAbboKluztThJs=) Peter,

As we endure the daily barrage of chaos and corruption from the second Trump White House, here's a reminder:

The rage, fear, and sadness we feel is only part of the story. Finding opportunity in defeat is what has kept me doing this work for 40 years and counting.

Here's what I know for sure: We're not going to sit by while Trump tries to destroy our democracy. We will capitalize on our anger, we will fight back, and we will win.

**I want to tell you a story from the early days of EMILYs List that I've been thinking about a lot lately. It's about defeat, hope, and what comes next:**

In 1992, in the wake of Anita Hill's bravery during Clarence Thomas' Supreme Court hearings, women nationwide rose up and turned out in _historic numbers_ to elect Democratic women _. _EMILYs List's membership grew from 3,000 to 24,000 that year. We raised $6.5 million for our candidates. **And we elected four Democratic women to the U.S. Senate and 20 to the U.S. House.** It was an extraordinary, record-shattering year. After all that success, we thought we could accomplish _anything_.

And then came 1994.

Newt Gingrich rolled out the GOP's "Contract with America" legislative platform, seeking to nationalize the midterm elections. And it worked. Republicans picked up 54 U.S. House seats and 10 U.S. Senate seats, taking control of both chambers. We lost some of the women we had just worked _so hard_ to elect in 1992.

It was dubbed the "Republican Revolution." And in the aftermath, all the media wanted to talk about was how voters were sick of Democrats, how we had alienated the white men in our base, and how the American people no longer believed Democrats could deliver for them. It was a real moment of reckoning for our party. (Sound familiar?)

So here's what we did: We doubled down. We launched our "Boot Newt" campaign to combat the new speaker's extremist agenda, and we fought voter apathy by going straight to the press to let Americans know exactly what we stood for. We created the EMILYs List major donor program, which has gone on to raise hundreds of millions of dollars. We built a powerful political program that directly assists our women in launching and running their campaigns. And we launched Women Vote, our massively successful independent expenditure arm, which has helped elect hundreds of Democratic pro-choice women over the past 30 years.

**1992 is the year that put EMILYs List on the map. But if it weren't for the losses of 1994, EMILYs List may not have become the powerhouse institution it is today.**

The business of making real social change through electoral politics isn't easy, and it isn't linear. I call it "leaping and creeping" — we make big leaps forward, and then we face some tough losses, and then we creep forward again, win by win. As we celebrate 40 years of EMILYs List, I'm here to tell you, Peter: _We're always moving forward_. Even when it feels like all is lost. _Over time, we are making real and lasting change_. That's the story of EMILYs List, it's the story of the Democratic Party, and it's the story of America.

There's no way around it: The Trump administration poses an existential threat to our rights and our democracy. And while there are many reasons for fear, outrage, and disappointment, there are just as many reasons for hope. Together, we will rebuild and recover. The next leap is around the corner.

Thank you for being with EMILYs List, today and always.

Ellen Malcolm
Founder, EMILYs List

clean_body: | = Peter, As we endure the daily barrage of chaos and corruption from the second Trump White House, here's a reminder: The rage, fear, and sadness we feel is only part of the story. Finding opportunity in defeat is what has kept me doing this work for 40 years and counting. Here's what I know for sure: We're not going to sit by while Trump tries to destroy our democracy. We will capitalize on our anger, we will fight back, and we will win. I want to tell you a story from the early days of EMILYs List that I've been thinking about a lot lately. It's about defeat, hope, and what comes next: In 1992, in the wake of Anita Hill's bravery during Clarence Thomas' Supreme Court hearings, women nationwide rose up and turned out in historic numbers to elect Democratic women . EMILYs List's membership grew from 3,000 to 24,000 that year. We raised $6.5 million for our candidates. And we elected four Democratic women to the U.S. Senate and 20 to the U.S. House. It was an extraordinary, record-shattering year. After all that success, we thought we could accomplish anything. And then came 1994. Newt Gingrich rolled out the GOP's "Contract with America" legislative platform, seeking to nationalize the midterm elections. And it worked. Republicans picked up 54 U.S. House seats and 10 U.S. Senate seats, taking control of both chambers. We lost some of the women we had just worked so hard to elect in 1992. It was dubbed the "Republican Revolution." And in the aftermath, all the media wanted to talk about was how voters were sick of Democrats, how we had alienated the white men in our base, and how the American people no longer believed Democrats could deliver for them. It was a real moment of reckoning for our party. (Sound familiar?) So here's what we did: We doubled down. We launched our "Boot Newt" campaign to combat the new speaker's extremist agenda, and we fought voter apathy by going straight to the press to let Americans know exactly what we stood for. We created the EMILYs List major donor program, which has gone on to raise hundreds of millions of dollars. We built a powerful political program that directly assists our women in launching and running their campaigns. And we launched Women Vote, our massively successful independent expenditure arm, which has helped elect hundreds of Democratic pro-choice women over the past 30 years. 1992 is the year that put EMILYs List on the map. But if it weren't for the losses of 1994, EMILYs List may not have become the powerhouse institution it is today. The business of making real social change through electoral politics isn't easy, and it isn't linear. I call it "leaping and creeping" — we make big leaps forward, and then we face some tough losses, and then we creep forward again, win by win. As we celebrate 40 years of EMILYs List, I'm here to tell you, Peter: We're always moving forward. Even when it feels like all is lost. Over time, we are making real and lasting change. That's the story of EMILYs List, it's the story of the Democratic Party, and it's the story of America. There's no way around it: The Trump administration poses an existential threat to our rights and our democracy. And while there are many reasons for fear, outrage, and disappointment, there are just as many reasons for hope. Together, we will rebuild and recover. The next leap is around the corner. Thank you for being with EMILYs List, today and always. Ellen Malcolm Founder, EMILYs List = | = = = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. THE NRCC OUTRAISED DEMOCRATS LAST QUARTER, AND THEY'RE ON TRACK TO DO THE SAME THIS QUARTER
The GOP raised $36.7 million last quarter, folks.
They are NOT playing around.
Republicans aren’t just hoping to hold the House. They’re planning to straight-up purchase it.
The NRCC is quietly flooding battleground districts with dark money, billionaire cash, and far-right talking points — all in an effort to lock in GOP control of Congress before most voters have even tuned in.
We must match their war chest dollar for dollar - and thanks to the strength of our grassroots movement, we can.
Because when everyday Democrats chip in $5, $20, or $50 at a time, we create something stronger than any billionaire super PAC: people-powered political muscle that can flip seats, win races, and fight back against MAGA extremism.

Peter,

We’re the Schrier Finance Team, and we’re the ones crunching the numbers every single deadline we face.

That’s because the budget we prepared for this campaign depends on your support, and right now, we’re falling short of our FEC end-of-quarter goal with less than 24 hours to go. I won’t back down while Donald Trump is threatening the safety and security of Californians, while he’s making it harder for families to make ends meet.

Congressional Republicans are following Trump’s marching orders and making drastic cuts to Medicaid, NIH, SNAP, affordable housing, Pell grants, and more. The GOP is doing everything in its power to overwhelm us, so we give up. **_But we can’t let them win._**

I am fighting for our families, friends, and neighbors. I need your help to sustain this fight.

The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. I just want to play fetch and have my belly rubbed – is that too much to ask, doggone it? He’s already so busy making calls and doing his darndest to protect all of us from Donald Trump. Can you help us out so he can continue to focus on delivering on his promises to you (and get back to hanging out with me)? = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = I’m pawsitive it’s a win-win for everyone! = Lots of wags, Blue Official Best Friend of Rep. Raul Ruiz | | | ---|---|--- = The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.** | = = PAID FOR BY DR. ## Ten Commandments Posted in Public Does Not Establish Religion
When Texas Gov. Greg Abbott was serving as his state’s attorney general in 2005, he appeared before the Supreme Court to argue Van Orden v. Perry. The question was whether a monument inscribed with the Ten Commandments that had stood on the grounds of the Texas state Capitol since 1961 violated the First Amendment. subject: DEADLINE APPROACHING: Peter’s 3X match
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI2NTM5NTYS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjI0MTA1Mjg2OAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
The Senate vote on Trump’s Big Ugly Bill selling off public lands could come any day. Your LCV membership is more vital than ever. Renew now for just $5 to power our fight to keep public lands in public hands. Your gift will be TRIPLED until tomorrow’s midnight deadline >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/yAyZMNmb_- Peter — the Senate is voting soon on Trump’s public lands sell-off.
Just days ago, the Senate proposed a provision in the bill that would force the sale of public lands— opening the door to drilling, mining, and development in public lands
including sensitive areas of the Arctic and sites sacred to Indigenous peoples. We’re running out of time to mobilize in support of public lands — and with our
Summer Membership Drive ending tomorrow night at midnight, your support is more crucial than ever. The impact of this bill could reverberate for generations to come. We need to
respond NOW before Trump signs their plan to sell off public lands, cut our
healthcare, and destroy clean energy programs into law. We urgently need environmental advocates like you to renew your membership before our TRIPLE MATCH offer expires tomorrow. Renew now for just $5 to stop extremists from giving away our public lands to polluters >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/x_yzTg_ LEAGUE OF CONSERVATION VOTERS
SUMMER MEMBERSHIP DRIVE

Fundraising Goal: $40,000
Deadline: MIDNIGHT TOMORROW
Status: GOAL NOT MET — RENEW FOR $5 NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/KybpT-

Supporter Name: Peter Willis
Email Address: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Member Status: PENDING — RENEW FOR $5 NOW >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/_4bqMENW_ Passing this bill is just the beginning as Republicans use every possible avenue
to hand even more power over to corporations and polluters: The Trump administration just announced they are rescinding protections for tens of millions of acres of national forests, which could destroy forests that are critical to tackling the climate crisis and providing clean water and air to millions of people.
The Trump EPA is working to override the Clean Air Act, rolling back air pollution standards and letting fossil fuel companies get away with poisoning the air we breathe.
The Supreme Court is working to limit your right to weigh in on projects that directly impact your family’s health, allowing corporations to have the final say in whether their toxic projects are harmful to communities. Peter, it’s Ritchie. I’ll make this quick:

**_I’ve told you about me:_**
→ I was raised by a single mother in the Bronx who kept my family afloat on minimum wage, which was just over $4 at the time.
→ I grew up in public housing ridden with mold, leaks, vermin, and without consistent heat or water.
→ My path to Congress was untraditional, but I’m grateful to bring the wisdom of lived experience to my role as a public servant.

**_I’ve told you what I’m fighting for:_**
→ Affordable housing and building social safety nets to eliminate poverty.
→ LGBTQ+ equality.
→ Social Security, Medicaid, and Medicare.
→ Expanding access to physical and mental health care, and more.

**_Now, Peter, I’m hoping you’ll join my fight to protect my seat and flip the House:_**

[Will you step up and chip in $3 before midnight to join me in this must-win fight for our Democracy before Monday’s Mid-Year goal? name: Team ODP
subject: 700,000 Ohioans could lose Medicaid while the Browns get a new stadium.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
This is what the Ohio GOP is doing with your tax dollars.
|
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507105/554560875/1801948859?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_EOQ3_250628_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=igHZ65j-R4eWt4HvkJ9RixIXVTipJi06wRfyupMFJmI=) Team, here’s what just happened in Ohio:

**The billionaire owner of the Cleveland Browns – who, by the way, has donated more than $100,000 to statehouse Republicans – is getting a brand new stadium... and making Ohio taxpayers foot the bill.**

Meanwhile... over 700,000 Ohioans are at risk of losing Medicaid coverage under the GOP’s newly passed state budget, a bill Republicans are rushing to the governor’s desk _right now_.

[**_If that’s all you need to hear, chip in ahead of our end-of-quarter fundraising deadline to elect Ohio Democrats who will fight for a state that works for ALL of us, not just the powerful few >>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507107/554560879/1801948859?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_EOQ3_250628_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=igHZ65j-R4eWt4HvkJ9RixIXVTipJi06wRfyupMFJmI=)

Let’s be crystal clear: Ohio Republicans are choosing billionaire donors over working families. They’re choosing luxury suites over life-saving healthcare. And they’re proud of it.

This budget is devastating for working families in Ohio. It defunds our schools and libraries. It guts rural hospitals. It threatens healthcare for hundreds of thousands of families. And it hands the rich and powerful _more tax cuts_.

This is _exactly_ why our end-of-quarter deadline and our $20,000 grassroots fundraising goal matters so much.

Because after tomorrow night, we have to file a public report showing how much support we’ve built this quarter. If we fall short, Republicans will see it as a sign of weakness and jump on the opportunity to outspend and attack us in key districts so they can expand their foothold of power and keep pushing cruel policies.

[**_So we’re asking: will you pitch in before our deadline and help us hit our $20,000 fundraising goal?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507109/554560882/1801948859?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_EOQ3_250628_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=igHZ65j-R4eWt4HvkJ9RixIXVTipJi06wRfyupMFJmI=)

Thank you for being in this fight with us,

Ohio Dems **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507111/554560887/1801948859?refcode=ODP_ATA_EM_FR_EOQ3_250628_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=igHZ65j-R4eWt4HvkJ9RixIXVTipJi06wRfyupMFJmI=)**

It guts rural hospitals. It threatens healthcare for hundreds of thousands of families. And it hands the rich and powerful more tax cuts. This is exactly why our end-of-quarter deadline and our $20,000 grassroots fundraising goal matters so much. Because after tomorrow night, we have to file a public report showing how much support we’ve built this quarter. If we fall short, Republicans will see it as a sign of weakness and jump on the opportunity to outspend and attack us in key districts so they can expand their foothold of power and keep pushing cruel policies. = Thank you for being in this fight with us, Ohio Dems = | Ohio Democratic Party PO Box 130 Columbus, Ohio 43216 | Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | Click here to from the Ohio Democratic Party's email list.

name: Emily Randall
subject: What Pride means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507073/554560815/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.06.29_EOQ3Pride_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BciI0r5EGxTFgbQ8i3JzXLkc4Kuz38FrCvkQ4PIZBNM=&emci=1989afd4-9253-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=428ce703-2c55-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=23483442) | Peter— Seattle Pride was the first Pride event I ever attended. Twenty years ago, I had just come out, and I decided to volunteer at the festival while I was home from college. Today, I led the Seattle Pride parade as its Grand Marshall. It was a full-circle moment that I think my 19-year-old self would be proud of. Today’s celebration was also a powerful reminder of just how important and impactful it is to be with community. Queer joy is an act of resistance, something worth celebrating and nurturing, perhaps more now than ever. Since my very first Pride, so much has changed. Our region, like many others, has seen a proliferation of Pride celebrations and Pride clubs and GSAs in schools – young people today have grown up with marriage equality as the law of the land. But today queer communities everywhere are facing attack after attack. 597 anti-LGBTQ bills have been introduced in state legislatures across the US in the first six months of this year. The Republican-controlled Supreme Court just upheld Tennessee’s Ban on health care for trans Americans. The Trump Administration is withholding federal funding from states that do safeguard queer communities. These are serious threats. As the first openly LGBTQ+ person to represent Washington in Congress, and a co-chair of the Equality Caucus, I am doing everything in my power to fight back. We won’t let MAGA extremists write the rules for our future. Not when we have the power to shape it ourselves, not when we fight on with hope and determination. I was honored to sit down with Bree Coven from the _Seattle Times_ a few weeks back to talk about this fight, what Pride means to me, and more. [_**You can read the full piece here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/110507075/554560817/1932879312?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.06.29_EOQ3Pride_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MjhjZTcwMy0yYzU1LWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BciI0r5EGxTFgbQ8i3JzXLkc4Kuz38FrCvkQ4PIZBNM=&emci=1989afd4-9253-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=428ce703-2c55-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=23483442)
